description: Host recon and privilege escalation attack preconditions catalog. Evidence-ranked conditions derived from SSH/WinRM collection, Nmap scans, and Metasploit session data.

HO-01.label: host_reachable_and_responsive
HO-01.description: Target host responds to network probes; collection or exploitation is feasible.
HO-01.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 4 (nmap scan or successful TCP connect); rank 1 after successful SSH/WinRM authentication

HO-02.label: ssh_service_exposed
HO-02.description: SSH service is listening on a reachable port.
HO-02.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 4 (nmap service scan or banner grab); rank 1 after successful SSH authentication

HO-03.label: ssh_credential_valid
HO-03.description: A valid SSH credential (key or password) for a user account is available.

HO-04.label: winrm_service_exposed
HO-04.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 4 (nmap port scan); rank 1 after successful WinRM authentication

HO-05.label: winrm_credential_valid
HO-05.description: A valid WinRM credential is available for the target.

HO-06.label: sudo_misconfigured
HO-06.description: sudoers grants NOPASSWD or wildcard exec for non-root user.
HO-06.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 3 (sudo -l output parsed from host)

HO-07.label: suid_binary_present
HO-07.description: A SUID binary that can be abused for privilege escalation exists on the filesystem.

HO-08.label: world_writable_cron_or_service
HO-08.description: A cron job, systemd unit, or service executable is world-writable or writable by a low-privilege user.
HO-08.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 3 (crontab / systemctl / filesystem permission scan)

HO-09.label: credential_in_environment
HO-09.description: A credential, token, or secret is present in environment variables, shell history, or config files.

HO-10.label: user_is_local_admin_or_root
HO-10.description: The collected user account has root/Administrator-level privileges on the host.
HO-10.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 1 (id output shows uid=0 or group membership)

HO-11.label: vulnerable_package_installed
HO-11.description: An installed package has a known CVE with public exploit code.
HO-11.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 2 (package list cross-referenced with CVE data)

HO-12.label: unpatched_kernel
HO-12.description: Kernel version is below a patch threshold that fixes known LPE CVEs.

HO-13.label: ssh_private_key_accessible
HO-13.description: An SSH private key readable by the current user exists in ~/.ssh or accessible paths.

HO-14.label: writable_authorized_keys
HO-14.description: An authorized_keys file on the host or an upstream host is writable.

HO-15.label: container_runtime_present
HO-15.description: Docker or another container runtime is installed and the current user can interact with it.
HO-15.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 2 (which docker, docker ps, /var/run/docker.sock permission)

HO-16.label: cloud_metadata_reachable
HO-16.description: Instance metadata service (169.254.169.254 or IMDSv2) is reachable and returns credentials.
HO-16.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 4 (HTTP GET to metadata endpoint)

HO-17.label: msf_session_active
HO-17.description: An active Metasploit session exists for this host.

HO-18.label: msf_credential_harvested
HO-18.description: Metasploit has harvested a credential (hash, plaintext, or token) for this host.

HO-19.label: smb_service_exposed
HO-19.description: SMB (445/139) is listening on the host.

HO-20.label: rdp_service_exposed
HO-20.description: RDP (3389) is listening on the host.

HO-21.label: nfs_share_world_readable
HO-21.description: NFS exports are present and accessible without authentication.

HO-22.label: password_reuse_likely
HO-22.description: The same credential found on this host is likely valid on other hosts in the environment.
HO-22.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 2 (credential correlation across MSF creds db or collection outputs)

HO-23.label: av_edr_absent_or_weak
HO-23.description: No endpoint detection/response agent or antivirus is running, or a known-bypassable version is present.
HO-23.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 1 (ps/service list, wmic product list)

HO-24.label: host_in_domain
HO-24.description: Host is domain-joined, enabling Kerberos ticket acquisition for lateral movement.
HO-24.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 2 (hostname, realm, domain field in collection)

HO-25.label: open_port_running_exploitable_service
HO-25.description: An nmap-identified open port is running a service version with a known exploitable CVE.
HO-25.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 4 (nmap -sV with version detection)

HO-26.label: smb_null_session_allowed
HO-26.description: SMB null/anonymous session permitted — unauthenticated share listing or enumeration is possible.
HO-26.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 3 (smbclient -N -L, enum4linux null session)

HO-27.label: smb_sensitive_files_accessible
HO-27.description: Sensitive or credential-bearing files are readable on SMB shares without authentication.
HO-27.evidence_hint: Evidence rank min: 2 (smbclient share traversal, grep for passwords/keys)

host_ssh_initial_access_v1.description: Gain initial foothold via SSH with a harvested or default credential.

host_winrm_initial_access_v1.description: Gain initial foothold on a Windows host via WinRM with a valid credential.

host_linux_privesc_sudo_v1.description: Escalate from low-privilege user to root via misconfigured sudo.

host_linux_privesc_suid_v1.description: Escalate to root via abuse of a SUID binary (e.g. vim, find, python).

host_linux_privesc_kernel_v1.description: Escalate to root via a known kernel LPE vulnerability.

host_credential_access_env_v1.description: Harvest credentials from environment variables, shell history, or config files.

host_lateral_ssh_key_v1.description: Move laterally using an SSH private key found on the compromised host.

host_container_escape_docker_v1.description: Escape container environment via Docker socket access on host.

host_cloud_credential_imds_v1.description: Retrieve cloud IAM credentials from the instance metadata service.

host_msf_post_exploitation_v1.description: Full post-exploitation capability via active Metasploit session: dump creds, pivot, persist.

host_network_exploit_v1.description: Exploit a network-exposed service running a vulnerable version (e.g. EternalBlue, Log4Shell, BlueKeep).
